coredata.dc:title: Highly selective detection of phosphate ion based on a single-layered graphene quantum dots-Al3+ strategy
coredata.dc:creator: Chen, Bin Bin
Sheng Li, Rong
Li Liu, Meng
Yan Zou, Hong
Liu, Hui
Huang, Cheng Zhi
coredata.dc:description: Determination of phosphate ion (PO4 3-) is important in biomedical and environmental arrays because its controlling concentrations are associated with different pathologies or the quality of water. Herein, we report a new type of photoluminescence (PL) probe for highly selective detection of PO4 3- based on a single-layered graphene quantum dots chelating with aluminium ions (s-GQDs-Al3+) system. The PL of s-GQDs can be enhanced by Al3+ through the aggregation-induced emission enhancement (AIEE) effect. With the addition of PO4 3-, the PL of the s-GQDs-Al3+ system is faded away because PO4 3- has stronger coordination with Al3+ which results in the elimination of AIEE effect and the decrease in the PL intensity of the s-GQDs-Al3+ system. Therefore, the s-GQDs-Al3+ system can behave as an on-off type PL probe for PO4 3- detection. It is found that the PL intensity ratio (I/I 0) of s-GQDs in the presence of Al3+ at 463nm is proportional to the concentration of PO4 3- in the range of 0.25–7.5μM with the limit of detection as low as 0.1μM. This selective assay has a great application prospect in the complex matrixes owing to its simplicity and specificity for PO4 3- detection.
coredata.dcterms:subject: s-GQDs-Al3+ strategy
Phosphate ion
“On-off” type
Selective detection

The PO4 3- detection mechanism with s-GQDs-Al3+ system
Sensitivity and selectivity of PO4 3- detection

xocs:funding-list.xocs:funding-text: We are grateful for the financial support from the National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC, Grant No. 21535006 , No. 21705132 ).

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: Morphology and optical properties of the s-GQDs. (a) HRTEM image (Inset: particle size distribution) and (b) emission spectrum of s-GQDs (Inset: s-GQDs solution under the 365nm UV lights lamp).
The biocompatibility of the s-GQDs. (a) Cytotoxicity test; the confocal microscopy imaging by excitation at DAPI of the s-GQDs in (b) HEp-2 cells and (c) HUVEC.

The mechanism of PO4 3- detection. (a) TEM image of s-GQDs-Al3+ system; (b) the PL spectra of s-GQDs in the presence of different kinds of metal ions; c metal ions: 25μM, c Al 3+, 20μM; (c) the PL spectra of s-GQDs in the presence of Al3+ (20μM) and PO4 3- (7.5μM); (d) the change of hydrodynamic size in the presence of Al3+ and PO4 3-. EX: 330nm; c s-GQDs: 25μg/mL; pH: 5.4 (CH3COOH/CH3COONa buffer).

(a) PL restoration of the s-GQDs in the presence of different concentrations of Al3+ ; (b) PL restoration of the s-GQDs-Al3+ in different pH solutions (CH3COOH/CH3COONa buffer). I and I 0 are the PL intensities of s-GQDs in the presence and absence of Al3+. (c) Relationship between PL intensity and incubation time of enhancing; (d) Relationship between PL intensity and incubation time of restoration. EX: 330nm; c s-GQDs: 25μg/mL; pH: 5.4 (CH3COOH/CH3COONa buffer).

The sensitivity and selectivity of PO4 3- detection by using s-GQDs-Al3+ system. (a) Sensitivity of PO4 3- detection; (b) selectivity of PO4 3- detection. The columns represent: 1, control; 2, PO4 3-; 3, Br-; 4, CH3COO-; 5, Cl-; 6, F-; 7, HCO3 -; 8, I-; 9, IO3 -; 10, NO2 -; 11, S2-; 12, S2O3 2-; 13, S2O8 2-; 14, SCN-; 15, SO3 2-; 16, SO4 2-; Concentrations of all the anions and Al3+ are 25µM and 20µM, respectively. EX: 330nm; c s-GQDs: 25μg/mL; pH: 5.4 (CH3COOH/CH3COONa buffer).
Schematic illustration of the highly selective detection of PO4 3- by using s-GQDs-Al3+ strategy.
The comparison of the determination of PO4 3-.

head.ce:title: Highly selective detection of phosphate ion based on a single-layered graphene quantum dots-Al3+ strategy

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: Key Laboratory on Luminescence and Real-Time Analytical Chemistry (Southwest University), Ministry of Education, College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, China

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: Key Laboratory on Luminescence and Real-Time Analytical Chemistry (Southwest University), Ministry of Education, College of Chemistry and Chemical Engineering, Southwest University

ce:correspondence.ce:text: Corresponding author at: College of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Southwest University, Chongqing 400715, China.

sa:affiliation.sa:organization: College of Pharmaceutical Sciences, Southwest University

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: Determination of phosphate ion (PO4 3-) is important in biomedical and environmental arrays because its controlling concentrations are associated with different pathologies or the quality of water. Herein, we report a new type of photoluminescence (PL) probe for highly selective detection of PO4 3- based on a single-layered graphene quantum dots chelating with aluminium ions (s-GQDs-Al3+) system. The PL of s-GQDs can be enhanced by Al3+ through the aggregation-induced emission enhancement (AIEE) effect. With the addition of PO4 3-, the PL of the s-GQDs-Al3+ system is faded away because PO4 3- has stronger coordination with Al3+ which results in the elimination of AIEE effect and the decrease in the PL intensity of the s-GQDs-Al3+ system. Therefore, the s-GQDs-Al3+ system can behave as an on-off type PL probe for PO4 3- detection. It is found that the PL intensity ratio (I/I 0) of s-GQDs in the presence of Al3+ at 463nm is proportional to the concentration of PO4 3- in the range of 0.25–7.5μM with the limit of detection as low as 0.1μM. This selective assay has a great application prospect in the complex matrixes owing to its simplicity and specificity for PO4 3- detection.

Highly selective detection of phosphate ion based on a single-layered graphene quantum dots-aluminium ions (s-GQDs-Al3+) strategy.
fx1

ce:section.ce:para: Phosphate has been extensively used in modern daily life and industrial fields due to its superior properties. However, excessive amount of phosphate existing in the drinking or environmental water has a negative effect on biological systems and aquatic ecosystems [1]. Moreover, the phosphate level in water can also provide useful information about eutrophication and some other problems [2]. Therefore, it is urgent to establish a facile phosphate sensing approach for environmental monitoring and biological assays.

In comparison to traditional methods, including electrochemical methods [3–5], colorimetry [6], chemiluminescence [7] and plasma mass spectrometry [8], the fluorescence sensing approach has received considerable attention due to their excellent properties of high sensitivity, simplicity and low-cost. With this in mind, various fluorescence probes have been prepared for specific detection of PO4 3-, such as N, N-bis{3,5-di[(1-pyrenylmethyl)carbamoyl]benzyl}pyridine-2,6-dicarbamide [9], Rhodamine 6G [10], and poly(ethylene glycol) decorated bis(terpyridine) zinc coordination polymer [11], etc. However, most of them belong to the organic fluorescence dyes, which suffer from poor photostability, high toxicity and small Stokes shift. What's worse, the organic fluorescence probes show lipophilicity which, that is to say, it is soluble in organic solvents (such as dimethylformamide, acetonitrile), which lead to the biotoxicity and go against the detection of PO4 3- especially in the complex matrix. Moreover, the inorganic PL nanomaterials, including silver nanoclusters/metal-organic shell composite [2] and CdS quantum dots [12], have also been developed for PO4 3- sensing. However, many of them suffer drawbacks such as high cost and complicated labelling procedure.

It is obvious that the high-toxicity and poor stability of fluorescence probes in current methods greatly limit their further applications. To meet these challenges, carbon dots (CDs)，a new type of PL chromophores with good solubility, stable PL and excellent chemical stability have been widely applied in the analytical field [13–21]. Especially, CDs have been widely used for the detection of metal ions based on inner filter effect (IFE), fluorescence resonance energy transfer (FRET) or electron transfer (ET) [22–24]. However, little study for the determination of PO4 3- using CDs has been reported until now, thus designing a highly selective and sensitive CDs PL probe for PO4 3- detection will be a challenging research. In the previous study, the fabrication of the single-layered grapheme quantum dots (s-GQDs) with blue PL through an efficient self-exothermic reaction was reported [25]. The as-prepared s-GQDs present excellent properties such as good stability and water solubility. In addition, the s-GQDs have rich hydroxyl, anime groups and Schiff base (C˭N) structures which can specifically bind with Al3+ to produce aggregation-induced emission enhancement (AIEE) effect [25]. Importantly, the stronger binding ability of PO4 3- with Al3+, resulted in the elimination of AIEE effect of s-GQDs-Al3+ system, and then the decrease in the PL intensity of the system. Therefore, the s-GQDs-Al3+ system can be used as an on-off type PL probe to detect the concentration of PO4 3- in the range of 0.25–7.5μM with a limit of detection of 0.1μM. The working principle of the biosensor is shown in Scheme 1. In addition, the s-GQDs-Al3+ system for detecting PO4 3- in complex water samples is investigated.

Triethylenetetramine (TETA) and hydroquinone are commercially available from Aladdin Reagent Co., Ltd. (Shanghai, China). Hydrogen peroxide (H2O2), aluminium chloride hexahydrate (AlCl3·6H2O) and sodium phosphate tribasic (Na3PO4·12H2O) are purchased from Kelong Chemical Group Co., Ltd. (Chengdu, China).

The TEM data of the s-GQDs is performed on a Tecnai G2 F20 field emission transmission electron microscope (FEI, USA). The PL spectra of s-GQDs are recorded with a Hitachi F-2500 fluorescence spectrophotometer (Tokyo, Japan). Hydrodynamic sizes of s-GQDs are measured using a ZEN3600 dynamic laser light scattering (DLS) (Malvern, English).

As previously reported, s-GQDs are prepared by an efficient self-exothermic reaction at room temperature [25]. Hydroquinone (100mg) is first dissolved in 2.25mL water, following by 2.5mL H2O2 (20%). Dark brown s-GQDs solution is obtained within 5min after the addition of 0.25mL TETA. Finally, the proposed product is placed in a dialysis bag (MWCO: 500–1000) and dialyzed against water for 2 days to remove small molecules.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Cellular toxicity test of the s-GQDs
ce:section.ce:para:
The 1 × 105 cells per mL human epidermoid cancer cells (HEp-2) in Roswell Park Memorial Institute 1640 (RPMI 1640) medium supplemented with 2% fetal bovine serum are added to each well of a 96-well plate (100μL per well). The cells are first cultured for 24h in an incubator (37°C, 5% CO2) and for another 24h after the culture medium being replaced with RPMI 1640 (100μL) which contains different doses of s-GQDs (10μL). Then, following by removal of the culture medium, a mixture of 10μL Cell Counting Kit-8 (CCK-8) solution and 90μL RPMI 1640 medium is added to each well. The optical density (OD) of the mixture is measured at 450nm with a Microplate Reader Model after 30min. The cell viability is estimated according to the following equation:
Cell
viability
[ % ]
=
(
OD
treated / OD
control
)
× 100 %
wherein, OD control and OD treated are obtained in the absence and presence of the s-GQDs, respectively.

HEp-2 cells (1 × 105 cells per mL) in RPMI 1640 supplemented with 2% fetal bovine serum are added to culture dish (1mL). The cells are first cultured for 24h in an incubator (37°C, 5% CO2) and for another 8h after the culture medium being replaced with 900μL RPMI 1640 which contains 100μLs-GQDs solution (25μg/mL). Finally, following by removing the culture medium, the culture dish is washed with PBS buffer for third times. Then the cells are mounted with glycerol on microscope slides for imaging. For comparison, the s-GQDs are also used to image normal cells (human umbilical vein endothelial cells, HUVEC). The imaging process is the same as the above method.

For the detection of PO4 3-, the s-GQDs solution (25μg/mL in CH3COOH/CH3COONa buffer, pH=5.4) first mix with Al3+ (20μM); then, various concentrations of PO4 3- are added. After 5min, the PL spectra of the mixture solutions are recorded. To evaluate selectivity of the sensor for PO4 3-, interferents are added instead of PO4 3- under the same experimental conditions. All the PL spectra are recorded under excitation at 330nm, and the emission intensity at 463nm is recorded for quantitative analysis.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis and characterizations of the s-GQDs
ce:section.ce:para: The s-GQDs can be rapidly synthesized through an efficient self-exothermic reaction at room temperature based on our previous method [25]. TEM image ( Fig. 1a) shows that the size of the s-GQDs is distributed in the range from 2.5 to 6.0nm, with an average size of 4.5nm. Under the excitation of 290–390nm light beam, the s-GQDs show excitation wavelength dependent PL emissions, and the maximum emission is located at 463nm with the maximum excitation at 330nm (Fig. 1b). And it is clearly seen that the s-GQDs show good water solubility with the brightly blue-emissive PL (Inset in Fig. 1b).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Cellular toxicity and confocal microscopy imaging of the s-GQDs
ce:section.ce:para: The toxicity of the s-GQDs is assessed through the cell viability of HEp-2 cells using Cell Counting Kit-8 method. As shown in Fig. 2a, over 85% HEp-2 cells viability is observed after 24h incubation with the s-GQDs (0.05–50μg/mL), indicating that the cytotoxicity of the s-GQDs is relatively low. To reveal potential applications for biolabeling, cellular imaging capability of the s-GQDs is preliminarily investigated. The confocal image shows bright blue emission in HEp-2 cells (Fig. 2b). It is also found from PL image that the emission mainly locates in the cytoplasm region, suggesting that the s-GQDs can pass through cell membranes and enter into cells. Meanwhile, we also investigate the PL of s-GQDs in the normal cells (HUVEC). As shown in Fig. 2c, the brightness and the location of s-GQDs in the HUVEC are almost the same as that in the HEp-2 cells. Thus the s-GQDs cannot distinguish cancer cells from healthy cells.

ce:section.ce:section-title: The PO4 3- detection mechanism with s-GQDs-Al3+ system
ce:section.ce:para: In a previous study, we confirmed that the PL of s-GQDs can be enhanced by Al3+ based on the AIEE effect [25]. In the presence of Al3+, the monodispersed s-GQDs aggregate into amorphous masses ( Fig. 3a), resulting in the enhancement of PL. Importantly, only the Al3+ can enhance the PL of s-GQDs (Fig. 3b) because it can specially bind with s-GQDs through coordination and electro-static interaction. However, the enhanced PL of s-GQDs by Al3+ will recover in the presence of PO4 3- because PO4 3- have a stronger binding ability with Al3+ than s-GQDs (Fig. 3c). In the presence of PO4 3- to the s-GQDs-Al3+ system, it can decrease the PL intensity of the s-GQDs-Al3+ system for the elimination of AIEE effect and the dispersion of the s-GQDs which can be validated by the change of hydrodynamic size s-GQD-Al3+ complexes (Fig. 3d).

To achieve sensitive detection of PO4 3-, we optimize the systemic conditions, including the Al3+ concentration, pH of the buffer solution and response time. As shown in Fig. 4a, the PL enhancing changes with increasing Al3+ concentration. A small amount of Al3+ cannot enhance the PL thoroughly, while excess Al3+ will result in low sensitivity, because PO4 3- binds with free Al3+ in solution instead of Al3+ on the surface of the s-GQDs. Therefore, the optimal Al3+ concentration is determined as 20μM. Then, the effect of pH value from 3.8 to 6.4 in analyte solution on the PL of s-GQDs is investigated (Fig. 4b). The PL intensity ratio (I/I 0, I and I 0 are the PL intensities of s-GQDs in the presence and absence of Al3+) of the s-GQDs at 463nm on a gradual rise with the increase of pH from 3.8 to 5.4, then fall rapidly when the pH value is higher than 5.4, which is due to the hydrolyzation of Al3+. Taking this factor into account, pH 5.4 is recommended as the optimal pH value for further detection. Moreover, the enhancing process level off after 4min and the restoration process level off after 5min with the extension of the incubation time (Fig. 4c and d), indicating that the detection process is very rapid.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Sensitivity and selectivity of PO4 3- detection
ce:section.ce:para: We explore the feasibility of using s-GQDs-Al3+ system for PO4 3- detection. The enhanced PL emission of the s-GQDs by Al3+ is found to recover when adding PO4 3- solution, and PL intensity ratio (I/I 0) of the s-GQDs at 463nm is proportional to concentration of PO4 3- in the range of 0.25–7.5μM ( Fig. 5a). Under the optimal conditions, the limit of detection is estimated to be 0.1μM on the basis of the generalized 3σ method. In order to investigate the selectivity of PO4 3- detection, we examine the PL intensity changes in the presence of typical anions under the same conditions. As shown in Fig. 5b, the anions such as Cl-, SO4 2-, and SCN- have no obvious influence on the PL of s-GQDs in the absence of Al3+. Further addition of Al3+ to s-GQDs solution introduces a dramatic PL intensity increase due to the AIEE effect between s-GQDs and Al3+. Only PO4 3- can recover the enhanced PL of s-GQDs by Al3+, while other anions cannot. These results verify that PO4 3- has unique quenching efficiency and other anions have little influence on the PO4 3- sensing strategy. Compared with other methods ( Table 1), this approach is more rapid and facile and can be completed within several minutes. And this method is also label-free, and not requires the complex modification process. Owing to the good stability of s-GQDs, the relative standard deviation (RSD) of each sample below 2.1% ( Table 2), revealing that the as-established method has high reproducibility and precision. Compared with other approaches such as the electrochemical method, the proposed method has a better precision and recovery due to the good selectivity [3,5].

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of PO4 3- in water samples
ce:section.ce:para: The proposed method is applied in the detection of PO4 3- in different kinds of water samples because PO4 3- is considered to be the main cause of eutrophication. The recovery study, carried out on the samples spiked with standard PO4 3- solutions (1, 2.5 and 5μM), is used to evaluate the reliability and accuracy of the detection method we developed. The obtained recoveries of the two samples vary from 96.8% to 114.0% and 96.0–105.8% (Table 2), indicating that the potential applications in the detection of PO4 3- in the natural water. In addition, the proposed strategy owns a lower limit of detection (LOD, 100nM) than most of methods, which can well meet the requirements of practical applications. Therefore, the similarity, high sensitivity, good selectivity and high reproducibility make this method be a novel and important approach for the detection of PO4 3-, although the LOD of it is not the lowest.

In summary, a highly selective PL sensor based on s-GQDs-Al3+ system is developed for PO4 3- detection in water samples. This novel approach is characterized by rapid, low cost and label-free. Most importantly, the s-GQDs-Al3+ system has high reproducibility for detecting PO4 3- that presents a broad application prospect in terms of detection of PO4 3- in different kinds of water samples.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: We are grateful for the financial support from the National Natural Science Foundation of China (NSFC, Grant No. 21535006, No. 21705132).
